tool_args.label: Proposed 2024 Budget
tool_args.raw_text: Mr. Willens explained the proposed 2024 budget is $19.3 billion

payload.description: Mr. Willens presented the November Financial Plan and the proposed 2024 budget.

tool_args.label: COVID-era deficit note payoff
tool_args.raw_text: MTA will pay-off a $2.9 billion COVID-era deficit note (issued under the Federal Reserve Municipal Liquidity Facility program) on December 15, 2023.

payload.claim_text: MTA will pay-off a $2.9 billion COVID-era deficit note on December 15, 2023
payload.description: The note was issued under the Federal Reserve Municipal Liquidity Facility program.

tool_args.label: Operating Efficiency Target
tool_args.raw_text: MTA is committed to saving $500 million in recurring operating costs, starting with $100 million in 2023, $400 million in 2024, growing to $500 million in 2025.

payload.claim_text: MTA is committed to saving $500 million in recurring operating costs, starting with $100 million in 2023, $400 million in 2024, growing to $500 million in 2025

tool_args.label: Operating Savings Identified
tool_args.raw_text: $427 million in savings has been identified across operating agencies and incorporated into the proposed budget.

payload.claim_text: $427 million in savings has been identified across operating agencies and incorporated into the proposed budget

tool_args.label: Fitch Rating Upgrade on Transportation Revenue Bonds
tool_args.raw_text: on October 25, 2023, Fitch Ratings upgraded its ratings on the Transportation Revenue Bonds to A from A- and revised its outlook from Negative to Stable.

payload.claim_text: Fitch Ratings upgraded its ratings on the Transportation Revenue Bonds to A from A- and revised its outlook from Negative to Stable

tool_args.label: Office Supplies Procurement Amount
tool_args.raw_text: one procurement action for MTAHQ for $12.8 million

payload.description: Request for approval for award of an estimated quantity purchase contract for the as-needed supply and delivery of office supplies and copy paper to Staples Contract & Commercial LLC

warnings: metric_claim.value_unit is accepted as passthrough, but prefer unit. value_unit has only 9 accepted observations; submit one metric per measurement and use unit for the measurement unit.

tool_args.label: Market Basket Pricing Discount
tool_args.raw_text: The negotiated Market Basket pricing is, on average, 19 percent less than the unit pricing for the same items under the current contract.

payload.description: Negotiated Market Basket pricing discount vs current contract

tool_args.label: Non-Market Basket Discount Increase
tool_args.raw_text: The negotiated Non-Market Basket Discounts are, on average, 8 percent higher than the average Non-Market Subcategory discount under the current contract.

payload.description: Negotiated Non-Market Basket Discounts vs current contract

tool_args.label: COVID-era Deficit Note Amount
tool_args.raw_text: a $2.9 billion COVID-era deficit note

payload.description: COVID-era deficit note issued under Federal Reserve Municipal Liquidity Facility program

warnings: metric_claim.value_unit is accepted as passthrough, but prefer unit. value_unit has only 9 accepted observations; submit one metric per measurement and use unit for the measurement unit.

tool_args.label: Risk 1: Paid Ridership Recovery
tool_args.raw_text: Continued paid ridership recovery - lower growth than the projected 80% pre-pandemic ridership (versus the current 70%) could cost MTA $325 per year for 5% lower recovery.

payload.claim_text: Continued paid ridership recovery - lower growth than the projected 80% pre-pandemic ridership (versus the current 70%) could cost MTA $325 per year for 5% lower recovery
payload.description: First of five risks to the financial plan outlined by Kevin Willens

tool_args.label: Risk 2: Dedicated Tax Receipts
tool_args.raw_text: Dedicated tax receipts - a slower economy and/or softer real estate market could cost the MTA $250-$750 million per year.

payload.claim_text: Dedicated tax receipts - a slower economy and/or softer real estate market could cost the MTA $250-$750 million per year
payload.description: Second financial risk: dedicated tax receipt shortfall

tool_args.label: Risk 3: Casino Revenue Timing
tool_args.raw_text: Casino revenue timing - could be slower than anticipated, currently assumed at $500 per year in 2026 and 2027.

payload.claim_text: Casino revenue timing - could be slower than anticipated, currently assumed at $500 per year in 2026 and 2027
payload.description: Third financial risk: casino revenue timing slower than anticipated

tool_args.label: Risk 4: Operating Efficiencies
tool_args.raw_text: Operating efficiencies - if less than full implementation could cost $400-$500 million per year.

payload.claim_text: Operating efficiencies - if less than full implementation could cost $400-$500 million per year
payload.description: Fourth financial risk: operating efficiency savings not fully realized

tool_args.label: Risk 5: Capital Program / Congestion Pricing
tool_args.raw_text: Capital program remains funded – if the New Jersey litigation delays congestion pricing, assumes $100-$200 million per year in higher MTA debt service costs.

payload.claim_text: Capital program remains funded – if the New Jersey litigation delays congestion pricing, assumes $100-$200 million per year in higher MTA debt service costs
payload.description: Fifth financial risk: New Jersey litigation delaying congestion pricing

tool_args.label: Fitch Ratings Upgrade - October 2023
tool_args.raw_text: on October 25, 2023, Fitch Ratings upgraded its ratings on the Transportation Revenue Bonds to A from A- and revised its outlook from Negative to Stable

payload.description: Fitch Ratings upgraded its ratings on the Transportation Revenue Bonds to A from A- and revised its outlook from Negative to Stable

tool_args.raw_text: MTA will pay-off a $2.9 billion COVID-era deficit note on December 15, 2023

tool_args.label: Enhanced Ratings Upgrade - Oct 27 and Nov 8, 2023
tool_args.raw_text: on October 27, 2023, and November 8, 2023, the long-term enhanced ratings on existing subseries of Transportation Revenue Variable Rate Bonds were also upgraded.

payload.description: Long-term enhanced ratings on existing subseries of Transportation Revenue Variable Rate Bonds were upgraded

tool_args.label: Broadway Junction Station Accessibility Improvements
tool_args.raw_text: An acquisition of temporary and permanent easements from the New York City Department of Parks and Recreation in support of accessibility improvements at Broadway Junction Station in Brooklyn.

payload.description: Acquisition of temporary and permanent easements from the New York City Department of Parks and Recreation in support of accessibility improvements at Broadway Junction Station in Brooklyn

tool_args.label: Automotive Fueling Station Project - North White Plains
tool_args.raw_text: The acquisition of 4 Fisher Lane from 4 Fisher Lane Realty Co., LLC in support of the Automotive Fueling Station project in North White Plains, NY.

payload.description: Acquisition of 4 Fisher Lane from 4 Fisher Lane Realty Co., LLC in support of the Automotive Fueling Station project in North White Plains, NY

tool_args.label: MTAHQ Office Supplies Procurement Contract
tool_args.raw_text: one procurement action for MTAHQ for $12.8 million. This was a request for approval for the award of an estimated quantity purchase contract for the as-needed supply and delivery of office supplies and copy paper to Staples Contract & Commercial LLC.
